attempts.rejection_reason: bright diagonal receiver/top-face base-color strip remained visible between masonry courses
attempts.first_cause: A08 top-owner UV override replaced the A04 authored dark-transition routing on exposed base ledges
attempts.smallest_recovery: restore the A04 authored dark-stone UV zones only on upward base-transition faces below 35.2 cm; retain geometry and atlas bytes

attempts.rejection_reason: A04 authored transition UVs made exposed ledges and rubble tops read as flat gray without convincing stone detail
attempts.first_cause: uniform authored dark-stone atlas zones are suitable for hidden fill but not for camera-visible stone crowns
attempts.smallest_recovery: route only the same upward base-transition faces to verified source-owned C002/C003/C004 front-panel stone bands; retain geometry and atlas bytes

attempts.rejection_reason: bright source-background slivers remained on source-hidden receiver vertical faces
attempts.first_cause: A09 cardinal receiver re-projection reached source-background texels after the A10 depth reduction
attempts.smallest_recovery: retain source bands on visible crowns but route every hidden receiver face to the authored dark-stone fill zone; retain geometry and atlas bytes

attempts.rejection_reason: uniform authored receiver fill was too visible through the lower rubble gaps and lacked convincing stone detail
attempts.first_cause: the receiver fill zone was safe but visually under-detailed when camera-visible
attempts.smallest_recovery: route receiver faces to the bounded verified C002/C003/C004 stone bands by Z level; retain geometry and atlas bytes

attempts.rejection_reason: the owner-view perspective still exposed background through open receiver top loops and showed the full-height C004 receiver as a stretched lower skirt
attempts.first_cause: the inherited receiver topology had outer, inner, and bottom faces but no top cap; the C004 receiver also extended below the interface into camera-visible rubble gaps
attempts.smallest_recovery: close all hidden receiver top loops and limit the C004 receiver Z range to the C003/C004 interface; retain A10 exterior dimensions, source-owned atlas bytes, and all visible stone counts

attempts.rejection_reason: thin owner-view background slits remained below the closed receiver, between under-spaced C004 rubble stones
attempts.first_cause: the inherited 32-stone rubble layout retained too little tangential coverage after fitting to the A10 140 x 110 cm ovoid footprint
attempts.smallest_recovery: increase only local C004 stone tangential/radial coverage and minimum local height inside the unchanged 140 x 110 x 9 cm envelope; retain 32 stones, eight clusters, two rows, and the closed interface receiver

attempts.acceptance_reason: smallest sufficient A11 construction now preserves the authoritative exterior measurements while closing camera-visible assembly gaps without exposing a receiver skirt
attempts.next_gate: final render plus independent A11 audit; Flamestrike remains final visual authority
